findings.title: UI slider bounds enforced only at widget layer; persistence-side bounds missing
findings.description: Slider ranges in InferenceSettingsBottomSheet (temperature 0..2, topK 1..100, topP 0..1, maxTokens 256..8192) are enforced purely by Material3 `Slider.valueRange`. `buildOverrides()` writes the in-memory state verbatim to PerModelSettings and `DefaultAppSettingsRepository.savePerModelSettings` forwards it to DataStore without clamping. The proto promotion to `api` in core-settings/build.gradle.kts (protobuf-javalite) means any on-device consumer (future tools, debug builds, process-restore) can persist values outside these UI bounds — e.g. a negative maxTokens or topP > 1 — and `EffectiveConfig.merge` / `model.configValues` will propagate them to litertlm. No current exploit surface (the slider is the only producer today), but the classification is defense-in-depth because the proto is now part of the module's public API.
findings.impact: Low. A malformed value would at worst crash or destabilise litertlm in the same process sandbox; no data-integrity or cross-app exposure. Not reachable from user-controlled surfaces other than the clamped sliders themselves.
findings.recommendation: Add a single sanitize step in the repository before persistence, e.g. `settings.toBuilder().setTemperature(settings.temperature.coerceIn(0f, 2f)).setTopP(...).setMaxTokens(...).build()`, or a shared `PerModelSettings.sanitized()` extension co-located with the bounds. Keeps the UI ↔ engine contract authoritative in one place even as the proto becomes part of the public API surface.

findings.title: No length cap on user-entered systemPromptDefault before proto persistence
findings.description: OutlinedTextField in SystemPromptField uses `maxLines = 4` (visual wrap only — does NOT cap character count). The string flows verbatim to `PerModelSettings.system_prompt_default` (proto3 string = UTF-8, no schema-level cap) and then to `DefaultModelRegistry.resetConversation(modelName, systemPrompt = it)` which wraps it in `Content.Text`. Because DataStore replays the value on every process start and `applyEffectiveConfigToModel` runs synchronously before `registry.initialize` (ChatViewModel.kt:100-102), a pathologically large value (paste of a megabyte-sized blob) would be deserialised into memory on each app launch and passed into litertlm, degrading cold-start and potentially wedging the engine init path. Not a confidentiality/integrity issue — the field is self-inflicted per-user and the storage is private — but availability/robustness gap.
findings.impact: Low. Self-DoS at worst. No downstream interpretation of the string beyond litertlm `Content.Text` (no Markdown rendering surface for this field; SafeMarkdown from Task 6 applies only to message bubbles, not settings echo-back). No persistence poisoning across apps (DataStore file is in per-app `filesDir`, confirmed `allowBackup=false` + `data_extraction_rules.xml` excludes root).
findings.recommendation: Cap the TextField via a length guard in `onValueChange` (e.g. `if (it.length <= 4096) state.value = it`) and mirror the cap in repository sanitization. Anything in the single-kilobyte range is plenty for system prompts and keeps cold-start predictable.

findings.title: Proto-javalite promoted to `api` exposes raw proto types across module boundary
findings.description: `core-settings/build.gradle.kts` upgraded `implementation(libs.protobuf.javalite)` to `api(libs.protobuf.javalite)` so that `:app` can call `PerModelSettings.newBuilder().setXxx()` directly (used in InferenceSettingsBottomSheet.kt:97-105) and inspect `hasXxx()` in EffectiveConfig.kt:30-49. This couples UI code to generated proto types and the protobuf-javalite runtime, and makes the repository's public surface a proto message rather than a bounded domain type. Not a direct vulnerability, but it weakens the encapsulation that would normally keep wire-format serialisation concerns inside the settings module. A future shift to encrypted-at-rest storage, field validation, or proto schema evolution will need to touch every consumer.
findings.impact: Low. Architectural debt, not an exploitable flaw. The risk is that the next security-relevant change (validation, length caps, encryption) has wider blast radius than needed.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a plain Kotlin data class in `:core-settings` (e.g. `PerModelSettingsOverrides(maxTokens: Int?, ...)`) as the repository's public type, keep proto conversion internal to the module, and demote the proto-javalite dependency back to `implementation`. The merge / validation logic then has a single ingress point. Not urgent for Task 11 — track as tech-debt for the next settings change.

checks_performed.injection_system_prompt_path: Traced systemPromptDefault from OutlinedTextField → PerModelSettings → model.configValues → resetConversation(systemPrompt). The string becomes a litertlm `Content.Text` body — not interpreted as markdown, HTML, SQL, shell, URL, or code. SafeMarkdown/SafeUriHandler (D25, Task 6) guard message bubbles and outbound URIs respectively, neither is on the settings path. No injection vector found.
checks_performed.data_exposure_via_logs: Only new log call is `errorLog.e("inference-init", "heavy reinit failed", e)` (ChatViewModel.kt:386). ErrorLog component allowlist unchanged ('inference-init' was Phase-1). Description is a static 20-char literal; cause Throwable is bounded to MAX_CAUSE_MESSAGE_LEN=200 with control-whitespace sanitization. No settings values, no system prompt, no model config dumped. Existing 'settings-io' component used by DataStore error path for I/O failures only, with description `"save failed for modelId=$modelId"` — modelId is not secret. OK.
checks_performed.insecure_storage_datastore: app_settings.pb lives at `context.filesDir/datastore/app_settings.pb` — per-app private storage (Android MODE_PRIVATE equivalent for filesDir). AndroidManifest.xml:23-24 sets `allowBackup=false` and `dataExtractionRules=@xml/data_extraction_rules` (Task 5). data_extraction_rules.xml excludes domain="root" path="." from both cloud-backup and device-transfer — settings won't leak via backup. Device-local encryption is the user's responsibility (FBE / FDE). For an on-device LLM app with no sensitive credentials in these settings (numeric params + user-authored system prompt), this posture is appropriate. OK.
checks_performed.proto_api_exposure: Covered as a minor finding above. No leakage of internal-only fields — the proto only contains the 7 allowlisted settings, all intentionally exposed.
checks_performed.input_validation_bounds: Covered as a minor finding above. UI clamps; repository does not. No exploitation surface today.
checks_performed.toctou_double_apply: `applyHeavySetting` guards via `_reinitInProgress = true` at entry, `false` only in `finally`. `send()` checks `if (_reinitInProgress.value) return` (ChatViewModel.kt:123). UI bottom-sheet shows `ReinitProgressDialog` bound to the same flag (ChatScreen.kt:111-113). The modal dialog uses `DialogProperties(dismissOnBackPress = false, dismissOnClickOutside = false)` (ReinitProgressDialog.kt:27-30) so the user cannot dismiss and re-fire. However, `saveAndApplySettings` launches a fresh coroutine each call — two rapid taps on the Apply button (before `_reinitInProgress` flips to true within the launch) would queue two coroutines, the second of which enters `applyHeavySetting` after the first finishes, running a second cleanup+initialize cycle. In practice this is benign (second cycle finds identical persisted state and no-ops to the same result), but the gate is at the coroutine body, not at call dispatch. Acceptable; flagged here for completeness, not as a finding.
checks_performed.integer_overflow_nan: IntSliderField.onValueChange path: `kotlin.math.round(v / step) * step).toInt().coerceIn(range.first, range.last)`. Material3 Slider cannot produce NaN from a non-NaN valueRange bound to finite floats. `step` is hardcoded ≥ 1 at every callsite (temperature/topK/topP: step=1 default; maxTokens: step=256). `(range.last - range.first) / step - 1` in the `steps` calc uses Ints only, no div-by-zero. OK.
checks_performed.configvalues_stale_reference: `model.configValues = EffectiveConfig.merge(...)` reassigns the whole map reference. `send()` reads `model.configValues[ConfigKeys.ENABLE_THINKING.label]` once per call and captures into a local `accumulateThinking` (ChatViewModel.kt:139-142) before any streaming, so no mid-stream drift. `DefaultModelRegistry` consumers read via property lookup, not cached reference. OK.
checks_performed.hardcoded_secrets_scan: Grep on added files: no API keys, tokens, passwords, base64 blobs, or connection strings. Only string literals are i18n keys, UI labels ('GPU', 'CPU'), component identifiers ('inference-init'), format strings. OK.
checks_performed.dependency_scan: No new external dependencies — protobuf-javalite already present, only visibility modifier changed (implementation → api).

verdict: Approved. No critical or major findings. Three minor defense-in-depth observations around UI-only input validation (slider bounds + system-prompt length) and proto-api module coupling — none are exploitable in the current codebase, all are tracked as tech-debt recommendations for future settings-schema changes. Storage, logging, injection, and TOCTOU paths all check out: DataStore is per-app-private with cloud-backup + device-transfer excluded (Task 5 posture intact), ErrorLog enforces closed component allowlist + 200-char cause bound (Task 3 posture intact), systemPrompt flows to litertlm Content.Text only (no markdown/shell/URL surface), `_reinitInProgress` correctly gates `send()` and dismiss-blocked dialog blocks user re-entry. Safe to proceed to code-reviewer / test-reviewer.
